selection_notes: Included only CVEs whose primary failure mode is not memory corruption and whose root cause is not removed by Rust memory safety alone.

Prioritized core library code and shared libtiff parsing or directory-writing logic. Utility-triggered CVEs were kept whenever the vulnerable function lives in shared libtiff code; only wrapper-only CLI issues were excluded.
Included divide-by-zero, reachable assertion or panic, infinite-loop, resource-exhaustion, memory-leak, null-state handling, and checked-arithmetic, undefined-behavior, or input-validation bugs.
Excluded CVEs whose primary impact is out-of-bounds access, buffer overflow or underflow, use-after-free, double-free, or other memory-corruption classes even when triggered by arithmetic mistakes.

relevant_cves.issue: Directory parsing accepted a zero row-byte value and later divided by it.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Safe Rust still panics on division by zero unless the port validates dimensions and derived byte counts before arithmetic.

relevant_cves.issue: Zero YCbCr subsampling values reached strip and tile size math.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Safe Rust still panics on division by zero unless the port validates dimensions and derived byte counts before arithmetic.

relevant_cves.issue: Directory-entry pair fetching could follow an absent-data path and crash during field setup.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Rust removes raw null dereferences, but the same malformed-state assumption can still reappear as unwrap, panic, or incorrect error propagation.

relevant_cves.issue: A 16-bit loop counter iterated over a 32-bit strip count and could wrap forever.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Memory safety does not prevent non-terminating loops from malformed counters or directory structures.

relevant_cves.issue: Large directory offsets could bypass range checks through unchecked arithmetic and trigger assert-like failures in directory handling.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: This is a checked-arithmetic and representability issue; the Rust port must make range and shift invariants explicit instead of relying on implicit integer behavior.

relevant_cves.issue: The downsampled OJPEG vertical-flip path mishandled 64-bit image state during image extraction.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: This is a parser and state-management bug, not purely a memory-safety bug; the Rust port needs explicit validation and checked image-math logic.

relevant_cves.issue: Undefined OJPEG strip offsets could leave OJPEGReadBufferFill on an absent-data path.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Rust removes raw null dereferences, but the same malformed-state assumption can still reappear as unwrap, panic, or incorrect error propagation.

relevant_cves.component_hint: td_stripbytecount handling in shared read paths
relevant_cves.issue: An invalid td_stripbytecount value could leave shared read logic operating on absent strip metadata.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Rust removes raw null dereferences, but the same malformed-state assumption can still reappear as unwrap, panic, or incorrect error propagation.

relevant_cves.issue: Invalid ReferenceBlackWhite values were accepted in YCbCr-to-RGB conversion.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: This is a parser and state-management bug, not purely a memory-safety bug; the Rust port needs explicit validation and checked image-math logic.

relevant_cves.issue: Malformed OJPEG input could drive an assertion in post-decode handling.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Assertions on attacker-controlled input would still panic in Rust unless converted into recoverable validation errors.

relevant_cves.issue: TIFFVStripSize called TIFFGetField incorrectly, leaving invalid state that could reach bad size calculations or a zero divisor.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: This is a parser and state-management bug, not purely a memory-safety bug; the Rust port needs explicit validation and checked image-math logic.

relevant_cves.component_hint: compression capability checks in shared decoding paths
relevant_cves.issue: Image processing continued even when the required compression functionality was not configured.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: This is a parser and state-management bug, not purely a memory-safety bug; the Rust port needs explicit validation and checked image-math logic.

relevant_cves.issue: Codec-specific tags were not type-checked correctly when they appeared out of order in a directory.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: This is a parser and state-management bug, not purely a memory-safety bug; the Rust port needs explicit validation and checked image-math logic.

relevant_cves.issue: Ignored tags in the first parse stage left inconsistent state for the second stage.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: This is a parser and state-management bug, not purely a memory-safety bug; the Rust port needs explicit validation and checked image-math logic.

relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Safe Rust still panics on division by zero unless the port validates dimensions and derived byte counts before arithmetic.

relevant_cves.issue: Crafted TIFF input could trigger uncontrolled memory consumption before crashing.
relevant_cves.why_it_matters_for_rust: Rust still needs explicit caps on image dimensions, directory counts, and computed buffer sizes to avoid OOMs, hangs, and pathological allocations.
